title: Evaluating Online Information
subtitle: Think Before You Click!
overview: This session teaches students how to identify trustworthy online information, avoid fake news, verify websites, and become smart and safe digital learners.

images._note: Add screenshots of websites, fake news examples, trusted educational websites, and classroom discussion photos.

activity.title: Real or Fake Information Challenge
steps: Discuss which website looks more trustworthy
Challenge: Find one fake and one trusted website example

quiz.q: What should students do before believing online information?
quiz.explanation: Students should always verify information before trusting it.

quiz.q: Which symbol usually shows a secure website?
quiz.explanation: A lock icon near the URL shows a secure HTTPS connection.

quiz.q: What does HTTPS mean for a website?
quiz.explanation: HTTPS helps keep information secure while browsing.
Fake news spreads incorrect or misleading information.

quiz.q: Why is comparing multiple websites important?
quiz.explanation: Comparing sources helps confirm if information is accurate.

quiz.q: Which website is usually more trustworthy?
quiz.explanation: Educational and government websites are often more reliable.

options: A misleading title made to attract clicks
quiz.explanation: Clickbait uses exciting titles to attract attention unfairly.

quiz.q: What should students avoid sharing online?
quiz.explanation: Personal and private information should stay safe.

quiz.q: Which skill helps students judge online information wisely?
quiz.explanation: Critical thinking helps students evaluate information carefully.

quiz.q: What should you check in a website URL?
quiz.explanation: Checking URLs carefully helps avoid fake websites.
Some fake websites try to trick or mislead users.
Fact-checking helps verify if information is accurate.

quiz.q: What should students do if information seems suspicious?
options: Ask a teacher or trusted adult
quiz.explanation: Trusted adults can help verify suspicious information.

quiz.q: Which of these is a good online habit?
quiz.explanation: Checking sources helps students stay safe and informed.

quiz.q: What is the main goal of evaluating online information?
options: Become a smarter and safer internet user
quiz.explanation: Evaluating online information helps students browse wisely and safely.
